content.verdict: Selman Marrakech is the rare palace hotel that feels theatrical without ever tipping into kitsch. Jacques Garcia designed an Andalusian-Moorish fantasy in the Palmeraie, and the signature image — a 54-metre reflecting pool flanked by a stable of pure-bred Arabian horses — is as cinematic as anything in North Africa. For honeymooners, the combination of only 60 rooms, a Chenot-inspired spa with serious detox programming, and a black-tie dinner on the pool deck makes this our top pick for couples who want grandeur, privacy, and a proper sense of occasion. Come for Rachid Andaloussi's architecture and Garcia's interiors; stay for the way the staff remember your names by breakfast on day two.
content.best_room: Book a Pool Pavilion Suite with private plunge pool and garden. These ground-floor pavilions sit away from the main palace, give you a walled outdoor lounging area screened by bougainvillea, and include a large indoor living room with fireplace for cool Atlas evenings. Worth the upgrade over a Deluxe for the privacy alone.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Arrival and Palmeraie orientation
itinerary_7_nights.description: Private airport transfer, welcome mint tea and dates in your pavilion, golden-hour swim in the 54m pool, dinner at Le Pavillon with a Moroccan tasting menu served under the lanterns.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Medina immersion
itinerary_7_nights.description: Hotel car to Bab Agnaou, private guide through the souks, Ben Youssef Madrasa, long lunch at Le Jardin in the Mellah, and back to Selman for a couples hammam and aromatherapy ritual.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Arabian horses and stable tour
itinerary_7_nights.description: Morning tour of the stud farm with the equerry, meet the pure-bred Arabians, optional private riding lesson in the paddock, afternoon by the pool, sunset cocktails at the bar with live oud.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Ourika Valley day trip
itinerary_7_nights.description: Drive to the Atlas foothills, walk to the seven cascades waterfalls, Berber lunch in a riverside village, return to Selman for a private candlelit dinner poolside.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Chenot spa detox day
itinerary_7_nights.description: Full-day Chenot-inspired wellness programme: biocontactor therapy, phyto-mud wrap, couples signature treatment, light plant-based lunch, meditation in the garden, early night.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Majorelle and Yves Saint Laurent
itinerary_7_nights.description: Morning at Jardin Majorelle and the YSL Museum, lunch at Café Bastilla, afternoon shopping in Gueliz for rugs and ceramics, tagine dinner under the olive trees.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Slow morning and departure
itinerary_7_nights.description: Breakfast in the pavilion garden, final dip, late check-out at 16:00 arranged by the butler, farewell tea, private transfer to Marrakech Menara airport.

caveats: The Palmeraie location is a 20-minute drive from the Medina — romantic and quiet but you will rely on hotel cars for everything.
Summer highs (July–August) regularly hit 40°C, and even with the pool it can feel intense; shoulder season (March–May, October–November) is far more pleasant.
Some guests find the Garcia interiors dramatic to the point of heavy — if your taste leans pared-back Aman-style minimalism, preview photos of your room category first.

faqs.question: Is Selman Marrakech adults-only?
faqs.answer: No, it welcomes families, but with only 60 rooms and a large property the atmosphere rarely feels crowded. Honeymooners can request a quieter pavilion away from family-heavy zones.

faqs.question: How far is it from the airport and the Medina?
faqs.answer: Marrakech Menara airport is roughly 20 minutes and Jemaa el-Fna square in the Medina is 20–25 minutes by hotel car depending on traffic.

faqs.question: Is the spa really Chenot-branded?
faqs.answer: The spa is Chenot-inspired and uses Chenot methods and products for its signature programmes, including biocontactor therapy and phyto-mud treatments. Full multi-day detoxes can be arranged in advance.

faqs.question: Can we visit the stables even if we do not ride?
faqs.answer: Yes. Daily stable tours with the equerry are complimentary, and you can watch the horses being worked in the paddocks without booking a lesson.

faqs.question: Do they arrange private dinners for honeymooners?
faqs.answer: Yes. The team sets private dinners on the pool deck, in the olive grove, or in your pavilion garden with dedicated waiter and chef service, typically from around 250 USD per couple on top of the menu.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to visit?
faqs.answer: March to May and late September to November offer the best balance of warm days and cool evenings. December to February is quieter and cheaper but requires sweaters and fireplace nights.

We are planning our honeymoon and would love to stay with you for 7 nights from [ARRIVAL DATE] to [DEPARTURE DATE]. Could you please confirm availability and your best honeymoon rate for a Pool Pavilion Suite with private plunge pool, inclusive of breakfast and any current honeymoon amenities?

- Private airport transfer from Marrakech Menara (RAK)
